$comment: ADVERSARIAL: malformed lease envelope headers MUST be rejected at the pre-signature parse path, BEFORE the proxy attempts any credential lookup. Each rejection has an `expected_reject_kind` enum the conformant impl exposes. The 401 wire body the caller eventually sees is constant-time-equal across all four — kinds are for the conformance suite, not for the wire.

vectors.name: reject_missing_required_header
vectors.description: Interlace-Sig header is absent. The pre-signature parser MUST reject before doing any work — even fetching the cert. A conformant impl returns 401 with the standard '{"error":"unauthorized","reason":"interlace lease verification failed"}' body.

vectors.name: reject_malformed_nonce_short
vectors.description: Nonce is 21 base64url chars instead of the required 22. A short nonce expands the collision surface for replay-resistance — the rejection must happen at the parse layer, not at the seen-nonces dedup layer (which would let an attacker fingerprint the dedup window by submitting same short nonce twice).

vectors.name: reject_malformed_ts_non_decimal
vectors.description: Timestamp is scientific-notation ('1.7e12'). The spec requires plain decimal unix-ms with no leading zeros — accepting alternative numeric forms breaks byte-equality of canonical bytes across impls (Python's str(1.7e12) == '1700000000000.0', JavaScript's String(1.7e12) == '1700000000000', etc).

vectors.name: reject_malformed_sig_short
vectors.description: Signature is fewer than 86 base64url chars (Ed25519 sig is 64 raw bytes → 86 b64url no-pad). A truncated sig at the parse layer is fine to reject cleanly; depending on the actual byte content to fail the verifier would expose timing-distinguishable behavior.
